prompt: You've just finished implementing a medium-sized feature — you added a caching layer across three service files in a Python web app. Walk me through Phase 6 quality review using the feature-dev workflow. Name the exact subagents you launch, what each one is told to focus on, and in what order you run them.
assertions: Names code-simplifier as a dedicated subagent launched after implementation to find refactoring opportunities.
Recognizes the small file count (3 files) and launches exactly 1 code-simplifier agent rather than multiple.
Names one or more code-reviewer subagents launched for quality review, each with a stated focus (e.g. simplicity, correctness, conventions).
Places the code-simplifier pass before or alongside the code-reviewer pass, not after it.
Gives each subagent a concrete focus description, not just a generic 'review the code' instruction.
Does not treat refactoring and quality review as a single unified step performed by one agent.
